LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, MARCH-24-2020 HEARING RE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR. IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS IN DEPARTMENT 302 FILED BEFORE JUNE 1, 2020 ARE OFF CALENDAR PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S GENERAL ORDERS. TO RE-SCHEDULE THE HEARING, PLEASE REFER TO THE PUBLIC NOTICE TO RESTORE SERVICES ISSUED ON MAY 27, 2020 AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S WEBSITE AT HTTPS://SFSUPERIORCOURT.ORG/. (302/EPS) ***DEFENDANT REQUESTED TO TAKE DEMURRER OFF-CALENDAR PER EMAIL 3/17/20.***

LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

calendar.matters:
Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-28-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WARREN HAVENS' Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter is off calendar. On March 8, 2021, defendant filed a notice of appeal of the entry of the default and default judgment and that appeal is currently pending. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 916, "the perfecting of an appeal stays proceedings in the trial court upon the judgment or order appealed from or upon the matters embraced therein or affected thereby." This Court therefore lacks jurisdiction to entertain the instant motion to vacate default/default judgment since the propriety of entering the default/default judgment is presently on appeal. "A timely notice of appeal suspends the trial court's jurisdiction over the cause and vests jurisdiction in the appellate court." (In re Marriage of Varner (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 932, 936.)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Warren Havens.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gerardo C Sandoval. Clerk: Lyssette Bareng. Reporter: Nichole Rodich, CSR #11604. Telephone: 415-551-0616. Email: nrodich@sftc.org.
Ronald Lee Richman, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Bullivant Houser Bailey PC, A Professional Corporation.
Andrew Downs, partner and shareholder for plaintiff Bullivant Houser Bailey PC, A Professional Corporation, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Messrs. Richman and Downs appeared via ZOOM Video Conference for this morning's proceedings.
Defendant Warren Havens appeared telephonically via ZOOM Video Conference, was placed under oath and testified.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract; Amount Stated; Quantum Meruit was filed on January 29, 2019.
Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents, personally served on defendant Warren Havens on June 13, 2019.
Default as to defendant Warren Havens was entered on June 29, 2020.
There were no DOE defendants.

The Court took Judical Notice of the following:
Defendant Warren Havens' Motion to Quash Service and Motion to Strike Complaint is taken off calendar at the request of defendant on September 26, 2019.
Defendant Warren Havens "Motion to Quash of Summons or Stay or Dismiss" was denied on October 25, 2019.
Order denying petition for Writ of Mandate on December 27, 2019
Defendant Warren Havens' Demurrer to plaintiff's complaint taken off calendar per defendant's request on February 10, 2020.

Plaintiff Bullivant Houser Bailey PC, A Professional Corporation submit the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Bullivant Houser's Billing and Costs.......................................ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Bullivant Houser's Ledger Re: Calculation of Interest......................ID/EVID
Exhibit 3: Copy of letter from Ronald Richman to defendant Warren Havens..............ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Bullivant Houser Bailey PC, A Professional Corporation, and against defendant Warren Havens, as follows:

Principal/Damages - $896,630.01 (Amount pled in complaint).
Prejudgment Interest - $103,176.00
Attorney Fees - WAIVED.
Costs - $1,295.55.

Total - $1,001,101.56.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Petitioner stipulates to the return of the exhibits.
